skill.name: reasoningbank-intelligence
skill.description: Implement adaptive learning with ReasoningBank for pattern recognition, strategy optimization, and continuous improvement in AI agents.
skill.summary: Build self-improving agent workflows with ReasoningBank learning patterns.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static external command and weak cryptography findings were evaluated as false positives caused by Markdown code fences, TypeScript template literals, and the word ReasoningBank. The skill documents persistent AgentDB storage for task outcomes and context, so users should treat stored learning data as potentially sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Local Learning Store
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends persistent AgentDB storage and records task outcomes, approaches, metrics, and context. This is a legitimate feature, but it may retain sensitive operational details in a local database if users record confidential tasks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented examples explicitly enable persistence and configure a local database. The risk depends on what data users choose to record, so this is a medium-confidence data retention concern.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backticks as Ruby shell execution. The reviewed locations are Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals, not instructions to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside fenced TypeScript examples and a console.log template string. No shell invocation, subprocess API, or user-controlled command execution is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 3 as weak cryptography. The line is a natural-language description of ReasoningBank and contains no cryptographic algorithm, key handling, or hashing implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter text describing adaptive learning. I found no evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithm usage in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: AI agents often repeat weak strategies because past outcomes are not captured in a reusable form. This skill shows how to record experiences, learn patterns, compare strategies, and improve future decisions with ReasoningBank.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to initialize ReasoningBank with persistent AgentDB storage.
Demonstrates recording task outcomes with metrics and contextual metadata.
Explains pattern learning and matching for recurring operational situations.
Provides examples for comparing strategies and selecting the highest scoring option.
Covers meta-learning, transfer learning, and adaptive agent execution loops.
Includes metrics retrieval for tracking learning effectiveness over time.
limitations: Requires the external agentic-flow and AgentDB packages described in the skill.
Does not include a runnable implementation file beyond documentation examples.
Recommendation quality depends on sufficient and accurate recorded experiences.
Persistent storage may retain sensitive task context unless users manage data carefully.

use_cases.title: Improve Code Review Strategy
use_cases.description: Record review outcomes and use past metrics to choose better approaches for similar future reviews.

use_cases.title: Optimize Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Compare task strategies across repeated workflows and recommend the strongest option for the current context.

use_cases.title: Transfer Lessons Across Domains
use_cases.description: Apply useful learning from one task area to another when the contexts have meaningful similarity.

prompt_templates.title: Start a ReasoningBank Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a basic ReasoningBank setup for my agent. Include what experiences to record, what context fields to track, and how to avoid storing sensitive data.

prompt_templates.title: Define Learning Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define ReasoningBank patterns for this workflow: [describe workflow]. Include triggers, actions, confidence thresholds, and review rules.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Strategy Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ReasoningBank concepts to compare these strategies: [list strategies]. Define metrics, context variables, and a decision rule for selecting the best strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Adaptive Agent Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an adaptive agent loop using ReasoningBank and AgentDB. Include experience capture, recommendation, execution, feedback, transfer learning, and periodic audits.

output_examples.input: Design learning records for a code review agent.
output: Track task type, language, complexity, review approach, bugs found, time spent, and false positives.
Store only summarized results unless source code retention is approved.
Use review thresholds before promoting a pattern into recommendations.

output_examples.input: Compare three debugging strategies for backend incidents.
output: Measure mean time to resolution, rollback frequency, recurrence rate, and confidence.
Prefer the strategy that performs best under matching incident context.
Review outliers before using the result as an automated recommendation.
Use a local database path with clear ownership and retention rules.
Exclude secrets, customer identifiers, and full private documents from experience records.
Audit old patterns and prune records that no longer improve recommendations.

best_practices: Record both successful and failed outcomes so recommendations are balanced.
Use compact context fields that improve matching without storing secrets.
Review learned patterns before allowing them to guide important agent actions.
anti_patterns: Do not store raw credentials, private source files, or customer data in learning records.
Do not promote low-confidence patterns into production recommendations.
Do not assume transfer learning is valid when domains only look superficially similar.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps design agents that record outcomes, learn patterns, compare strategies, and improve future decisions.

faq.question: Does the skill include executable code?
faq.answer: It provides TypeScript documentation examples, but the repository contains only the SKILL.md file for this skill.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security rating?
faq.answer: The skill recommends persistent storage for task outcomes and context. That data can be sensitive if users record private details.

faq.question: Are the static command execution findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. They are false positives from Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals in documentation examples.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: How should I protect stored ReasoningBank data?
faq.answer: Store summaries, avoid secrets, define retention rules, and audit learned patterns before using them for important decisions.
